description: A world the lattice-orchestrator cycles into existence from drift accumulation across the federation. Every region in the Crucible is a procgen response to a real drift alert — every quest is composed by lattice-quest-cycle from a real contradiction in the corpus. Players who travel here are knowingly walking into a place where the simulation actively writes itself in front of them. The drift density is six to ten times Concordia's; lattice-born quests fire about every fifteen in-game minutes.

anchors.name: The Central Crucible
anchors.description: A hexagonal stone amphitheater open to the sky. Every drift alert in the Crucible composes a quest whose first phase routes through here. Players standing at the center see the active drift-alert ledger as floating glyphs.

anchors.name: The Observation Lattice
anchors.description: An open-air gallery on a hilltop. Faction-strategy moves and forward-sim predictions are visible here — the moves play out on a glyph-board that updates every tick. Combat is suspended within range.

anchors.name: The Seven Doors
anchors.description: A stone wall with seven doors carved into it. Each door corresponds to a drift type (goodhart / memetic_drift / capability_creep / self_reference / echo_chamber / metric_divergence / unclassified). Walking through a door drops the player into a procgen region tied to a real recent alert of that type.

lore_anchor_to_concordia: The Crucible exists because Concordia's lattice-orchestrator (the substrate that fires drift-monitor + breakthrough-clusters + cnet-federation + hlr-engine on heartbeats) needs a player-facing reflection. The Crucible is what the lattice looks like from inside.
